segment-text: Screenplay By
ART LINSON & JEFF ROSS and
EXT: 7TH AVE & 34TH ST, NYC - WINTER - DUSK

Snow and ice with big chill winds hit the streets but it doesn't slow down the throngs of New Yorkers heading toward the Long Island Railroad entrance of PENN STATION. The Camera settles on ONE MAN walking alone fighting the crowds and the weather. We meet JACKIE BURKE.
INT: ESCALATOR - LIR - PENN STATION - DUSK
Jackie, carrying a small sports bag, rides the escalator down into Penn Station, stoic, oblivious to the harried commuters around him.
INT. LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD TRAIN - LATER - DAY

Jackie sits alone on the train. He reads the NY Times, every once in a while circling an item, scribbling notes. An MIDDLE-AGED WOMAN across from him stares. Jackie offers a brief glimpse then turns away.
Excuse me? I know you . . . Don't I know you?
No!
Jackie returns to his notes. The Old Woman, speechless, goes back to minding her own business.
EXT: LIR HICKSVILLE STATION SIGN - DAY
The train pulls into Hicksville, Long Island.
EXT. GOVERNOR'S COMEDY CLUB - RAINY - LATE AFTERNOON A "Long Island Taxi" drives through a suburban shopping mall parking lot behind Petco, P.C. Richards, Staples,
bringing us to two small, isolated buildings: A tiny drive-thru "Dairy Mart", and a red cement-block bunker with the word "COMEDY" printed across the front.
INT. TAXI - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT

Jackie glances at the Marquee through the window... BRETT BUTLER from "GRACE UNDER FIRE" JACKIE BURKE from "Eddie's Place" JIMMIE "DYNOMITE" WALKER Jackie, expressionless, wraps his scarf around his neck and reads the computer screen: cash or credit.
(in rear-view mirror) I knew I knew you. "Eddie". You're "Eddie". My wife and I were just watching one of the old shows. She said you died. I says, no I think he's alive. We had a fight.
Jackie throws the driver a $20 and exits the cab. He runs through the pouring rain to the front door.
GOVERNOR'S COMEDY CLUB BAR - NIGHT
Jackie enters carrying his sports bag to find his female agent/manager MILLER waiting for hi.
Are you cold? You need something to warm you up?
You could torch the club so I don't have to do this shit.
Be prepared - it's not as big a house as they thought. I think the weather kept people home.
You sure it's not the marquee? Reads like Night of the Living
Dead.
INT. KITCHEN AREA - GOVERNOR'S CLUB - CONTINUOUS
Miller leads Jackie through a busy kitchen. Jackie strips off his peacoat as they pass the kitchen help.
I should fire you for this.
You said anything. You need money.

Jackie sets down his sports bag on a kitchen table, and Jackie starts shedding his winter garb: He takes off his hat and gives it to Miller. He unzips his sports bag and pulls out a pair of tennis shoes - kicking off his rubber snow boots, he slides on the loafers.
Oh, CBS passed on the pilot.
They give a reason?
They didn't like it.
You tell me this before I go on? What you do before you fuck your boyfriend? Watch a Holocaust movie?
At least I have a boyfriend. relationship do you have?
We can see the half-filled showroom through the kitchen door. We hear laughter from the showroom.
(points at audience) I've got them . . . the audience.
You want to say hi to JJ?
He walks out of the kitchen toward the showroom. Miller follows.
GOVERNOR'S CLUB - SHOWROOM - SIMULTANEOUS
JIMMIE "JJ" WALKER stands in the back of the half-filled room, watching Brett Butler performing on stage. Jackie & Miller appear through the kitchen door.
Good to see you, man. Thanks for coming.
Thanks for having me.
It's a small house but they're lively.
I only need one person to play. Sometimes I just play with myself.
Jackie B!

Dy-no-mite! Jimmie nods, and moves toward the stage as Brett Butler finishes her act. Finally, Jackie takes a small mirror out of the sports bag and hands it to Miller, who now holds all of Jackie's discarded cloths. She holds up the mirror so Jackie can arrange his hair.
She's still "UNDER FIRE", let's
hear it once again for Brett Butler! DY-NO-MITE!!!
You staying?
I'll drive you home.
Brett Butler exits past Jackie. They hug and shake hands.
If you ever see me at one these again shoot me in the head.
I'll do us both with one bullet.
Brett exits. ON STAGE:
So now please welcome a guy you might remember if you fought in World War II, from the sitcom
"EDDIE'S HOME"...a true black belt
The Audience is chanting, "EDDIE!
EDDIE!"
Jackie takes the mic from JJ Walker and puts on his show face.
Let's hear it for the great JJ

Jackie sizes up the crowd. He takes a deep breath and lets it out slowly, ending with a quick shudder. We'll come to recognize this as Jackie's tic when he's about to erupt into comedy -- a fighter coming out of his corner. The chanting grows louder: "EDDIE! EDDIE!" Jackie takes the stage. Grabs the mic.
Give it up for JJ WALKER. This guy
Jackie sizes up the crowd.
It looks like everybody here is with someone.
He steals a quick look back to Miller in the wings.
Lots of couples. A few singles. (@ some young women) What are you celebrating? Bachelorette party! Heather, I told you we should have gone to Chippendales! I don't care if they're all gay.
Okay, how many first dates in the house?
Crowd reaction.
Quite a few. (looks at a Table) (table nods) I thought so. (then to WAITRESS) Table 14 - separate checks. (audience laughs) How 'bout married couples? (several shouts) Oh, quite a few . . .
Jackie turns around so he's facing the Back Wall. He talks into the mic sotto voce - an internal dialogue.
Maybe I shouldn't do any marriage jokes. Everyone's is a good mood. They just saw Brett Fucking Butler, they're happy. I can't crush their world with bleak shit about marriage. Keep it light, right?
Jackie turns around and faces the audience.

(growling into Mic) Marriage will suck every speck of life from your soul until there's nothing left but one giant festering sore! No, really. I'm a big fan of marriage, Hicksville. I must be; I've been married four times. (MORE)

I don't think it's just me. I don't think human beings were meant to be together. Want a relationship? Get a canary. Get a fish. Don't get a cat -- that's too sad. But get a hamster. Get a dog. A dog's always happy to see you. A dog don't judge. Like when You're late?
Hey, Jackie, no big deal. WOOF.
You forget to feed them.
It's okay, Jackie, I'll manage.
You can even come home smelling like pussy, and they'll love you even more.
(sniff, sniff) Jackie, my man! WOOF WOOF... (sniff) ...Dawg, you smell great! You been into some cool shit. WOOF.

You can even bring another dog home. Sure, your steady dog might get a little jealous, but the worst he's gonna do is pee on your carpet, shit on the floor, maybe chew up a shoe or two. My third wife did all those things. But then she went and did what all wives do -- she got a lawyer. A dog will never do that. A dog will never go out and get a lawyer. And here's another thing a dog will never do. When a dog dies, it will not be resurrected 25 years later on "Nostalgia Night." Now I'm not suggesting anything, but if you get the right kind of dog--
Jackie is interrupted by a HECKLER seated in front with his BLONDE GIRLFRIEND.
(shouting) Enough of this dog shit! do Eddie!
Come on,
Not now pal, I'm working out some new stuff here.
Yeah, no, I didn't pay to hear new material. I paid to see "Eddie".
As before, Jackie turns his back to the audience, and speaks into the mic sotto voce - Internal dialogue:

Oh Christ, a heckler. This must be the infamous Hicksville Heckler I've been warned about. Big, loud, stupid ...I could destroy him, but I might lose the audience. They're on my side now. I'm on a roll. I'll kill him with kindness, go on with the bit, see where it leads. (turns back to Audience & Heckler) My friend, this is a place of peace where brothers and sisters gather together to nourish their souls with comedy and refreshments. You've come into
this, and-Now YELLING AT THE TOP OF HIS LUNGS
DESTROYED IT ALL YOU SNIVELING, FUCKING PIECE OF SHIT CUNT, YOU
SCUM SUCKING HIDEOUS FOUL DETESTABLE POOR EXCUSE FOR A LIFE FORM. YOU UNEVOLVED, LOATHSOME, VULGAR REPUGNANT ZIT ON THE ASS OF
Jackie abruptly turns around again to his "Internal Dialogue" position.
(yells from Audience) Hey. I'm the audience. You're here to entertain me! You work for me.
(to the audience) You hear that, I work for him.
He turns back to the Heckler.
(yelling) YOU SCROFULOUS, PUSTULAR, DISEASED
DICK OF A CUM CHUGGING, MOTHER FUCKING, SCAT MUNCHING, ASS LICKING PILE OF RANCID SHIT.
Jackie hears the Heckler speak triumphantly to his girlfriend.
You getting this all, Amy?
Jackie peers past the lights and sees a woman adjusting a GoPro Camera on a professional GoPro Steadi-Stick.
I don't want all that in our show.
Are you recording this?
Welcome to: (into Go Pro)
"STANDUP TAKEDOWN".
Jackie gets off the stage and walks towards to the Heckler's Table,
(walking) Webisode? You're making your own show?
(into the camera)
THIS IS STAND-UP TAKE-DOWN; JACKIE BURKE EDITION!
Really? You're recording my show?
This is my show! I did Carrot Top last week. Brought-him-down!
Jackie gets off the stage and walks to Heckler's table.
You went down on Carrot Top?
Heckler grabs Jackie's mic and faces Go-Pro.
Blow job jokes? A sign of weakness.
Jackie tries to pull his mic out of Heckler's hands.
Don't touch the mic.
(into the camera) This is Archie Severin challenging
Jackie Burke to a comic-face-off.
(pulling at mic) Let go of my mic.
(holds onto mic) Bet they'd rather hear my act than yours. I challenge you to a comicface-off.
(overlap) Not with my mic.
(overlap) What? You too chicken-shit to throw down with me?
OK, you want the mic - here.
Jackie uses the Heckler's own force to bang the mic against the Heckler's head and face...
OW...STOP
HERE! YOU WANT THE MIC ... YOU

WANT THE FUCKING MIC...EAT IT!!! JACKIE POUNDS THE HECKLER WITH THE MIC Blood spurts from the Heckler's nose, Jackie rips the mic out of the Hecklers hands and pushes him over the table onto his girlfriend, who screams. Jackie grabs the Go Pro and speaks into it in the cadence of his stand-up:

And if you do get a dog, don't take it to a comedy club. (turns to audience) I see my time is up. Thank you everyone. Don't forget to tip your waitresses, and be careful not to step in the puddles of blood on your way out. GOOD NIGHT!
The audience is in shock, but a few shout their approval. Jackie crosses to Miller and rushes out with her. CLOSE-UP:
DIGITAL IMAGE ON AN I-PAD
We see the shaky image of Jackie from the Go-Pro's POV, hitting the Heckler (MR. SEVERIN) with the mic. JACKIE (ON GO-PRO) You want the mic..you want the fucking mic..Eat it!!! The video stops.
INT. COURTROOM - DAY
Jackie's Attorney stands before the JUDGE, Judith Mansfield, showing her the video on the I-Pad. Prosecutor stands beside him also watching.
Your Honor, as you can clearly
see, Mr. Severin grabbed Mr.
Jackie sits at the defense table. Miller sits behind him. The "victim" (Severin) with a bandage over his nose and bruised eye, sits behind the Prosecutor's table.
Any objection to probation? With a strong Public Apology and substantial Community Service.
Nods to her Clerk. Attorneys return to their clients. Jackie's attorney gives him a "we won" thumbs up. Will the defendant please rise. Jackie stands.
Yes, Your Honor. And in accordance with the agreement, are you prepared to change your plea from 'not guilty' to 'guilty.'
Jackie looks at his Attorney who nods.
Yes, Your Honor.
The Attorney hands Jackie a sheet of paper. Jackie reads;
I admit to assault and battery on Mr. Severin. I recognize that I acted in a weak and violent manner, and I will aggressively take steps to ensure that it will never happen again.
Jackie glances over at the Heckler who smiles smugly.
I also apologize to the court and to my fans.
Do you have something to say to Mr. Severin?
(reluctantly) I'm sorry.
Severin leans across the railing and whispers to the DA, as if to say "that's not good enough". The DA turns and addresses the judge.
Excuse me, your Honor, but I think the defendant can do a little better than that. Mr. Severin could have suffered brain damage.
Mr. Burkowitz, you want to try that again?
Jackie looks over to Severin - who has a shit-eating grin, as if to say "I win, asshole". Jackie takes a deep breath and lets it out slowly.
No?
Miller covers her face. She knows what's coming.

No. You see, your Honor, there's no way Mr. Severin could have suffered brain damage because, according to the law as I understand it, it has not been proven in this courtroom that Mr. Severin in fact has a brain.
Shock and laughter from COURTROOM SPECTATORS.
Too late. Miller covers her face.

I mean, as soon as the guy opened his mouth, it was like Gorillas in the Mist - Look at him - You could tell by his face. You know, Judge, the look like Donald Trump fucked Alfred E Newman after a night of snorting thalidomide.
More laughter. The Judge pounds her gavel for order.
(to DA) Can he say that to me?
Look at him right now, struggling to understand what I'm saying. Stop right now. I'm warning you--
This is more than just about me, Judge. I think humanity's fate is in your hands. Remember, he was with a female that night. What if they breed?
Spectators laugh and cheer "You tell'em Jackie!". The Judge pounds her gavel. Prosecutor starts shouting. Severin is on his feet shouting.
30 DAYS in the Nassau County Correctional Center-TAKE HIM AWAY. The BAILIFFS roughly grab Jackie and lead him away. As Jackie passes Miller, he WINKS at her.

(continuing) One day there could be thousands of these one-eyed brainless idiots. It's up to you, Your Honor; the fate of the world is in your hands. Finish what I started. Save the world. Lock him up!
EXT. NASSAU COUNTY CORRECTIONAL CENTER - DAY
Rolls of Razor Wire glisten in the sunlight.
INT. CELL BLOCK - DAY

Jackie, in an orange uniform, is escorted by a prison guard into a double-tiered cell bloc. Two naked, totally tattooed Latino inmates take showers in an open shower stall. They eye Jackie - "New Meat".
(shouts to inmates) OK gentlemen, let's give a warm welcome to our newest resident.
Jackie hears from all the cells, inmates voices singing: "When your head gets in that cranky place...watch EDDIE'S HOME...EDDIE'S HOME...EDDIE'S HOME... Jackie walks into his temporary home:
EXT. NASSAU COUNTY CORRECTIONAL CENTER - DUSK 30-Days-Later:
Jackie, in same clothes he wore in court, walks down a long ramp. He holds a clear plastic bag filled with his possessions. Miller waits by her car.
EXT: QUEENS-SIDE ENTRANCE TO 59TH ST BRIDGE - DUSK
I wrote new material on the inside. Good stuff.
INT. MILLER'S CAR - BRIDGE - DAY
Miller drives as Jackie looks out the window;
I'd try it on you but you have no sense of humor.
No, I don't.
I have never seen you laugh.
No you haven't.
How can that be? You represent comics.

You just answered your own question. Can you imagine how fucking painful my life would be if I had to act like an audience with all my comics? "You laughed more at his joke, than ya did at mine" "You think he's funny, he's not funny". So I don't react to anyone and everyone feels equal.
And you call that personal management? Your father had affection for his clients.
Which his clients didn't have. He got you that TV show and you fired him. It was a William Morris package deal. Your father understood.
Just don't talk to me about affection.
I don't need your affection. You know what I need? I need to work. I want a New York club.
(rolls her eyes) Yeah, that'll get you out of the hole you're in - $30 a show at the Comedy Cellar ... You do that 540 times you might have the money to pay your attorney fees.
I'm hot now. I'm viral. Your father knew how to take advantage of opportunities like this... (with affection) "Millah the Killah" I used to call him.
He loved when you called him that. (beat) It killed him when you stopped.
Jackie looks out the window at NYC approaching.
EXT: 59TH STREET BRIDGE - DAY
Cars on bridge stream into the heart of Manhattan.
EXT. INDUSTRIAL BUSHWICK NEIGHBORHOOD - MORNING

HARMONY exits an old red brick building, dressed for the cold. Harmony stops, sees: MAC SCHUTZ, sitting in the back seat of a limo at the curb. Mac is in his late 60's with a jaw cut like a Marine, drenched in a Florida tan.
Harmony walks away down the sidewalk.
Mac gets out and catches up to Harmony, who is walking towards the elevated "F" train on Broadway. Jesus. It's 80 in Boca.
Harmony faces Mac, trying to contain her rage:
She turns and walks down Broadway to catch the El.

segment-speaker: A week from Monday's my birthday
segment-text: and you're having dinner with me. No ifs, ands or buts...
Mac smiles and sings to her like she was a little girl. LIFE...THAT'S WHY I'LL ALWAYS BE AROUND...YOU ARE THE APPLE OF MY EYE"....
Leslie runs across Broadway to catch the "F' Train. HEART..." Mac watches her run up the stairs to the "EL". gets back inside his Escalade.
EXT. BEN'S BEST DELI - LOWER EASE SIDE, NYC - DAY
Busy traffic jams the intersection of Norfolk Ave & Houston - even on Thanksgiving Day. Jackie leans against an icy wind as he ducks into a deli.
INT. BEN'S BEST DELI - CONTINUOUS - THANKSGIVING DAY

Jackie enters. On the back wall are several 8 x 10's of minor celebrities who have frequented the store, including a signed Jackie Burke photo in the center. JAMES BERKOWITZ - Jackie's younger brother - is working behind the counter. He sees Jackie and removes his apron.
Here he is. The prick who came in from the cold.
Place looks good. It smells more Jewish than I remembered.
James turns to his few customers.
My famous brother, ladies and gentlemen. Probably needs money.
Can I talk to you?
(surprised) I'm right? Jesus, I was kidding.
James leads Jackie to a tiny office in the back.
INT. JAMES' KITCHEN/OFFICE - BERKOWITZ DELI - CONTINUOUS
James sits behind his desk. Jackie takes a seat.
So, you need money?
No, no it's fine. I'm fine. I'm doing my community service at the Bowery Mission, so I'm in the neighborhood - Figured I come in and say hello.
Community service? Oh yeah, saw the video. How could you let a heckler get to you like that?
It wasn't the heckling. It was the goddamn camera. These fuckers were making their own show out of my show. It was nuts.
You looked nuts.
So how are you?
Good. You? Good.
Awkward Beat. James waits. Jackie hesitates. Then:
Look, the thing is, I lost a lot of gigs in the last four weeks cause of what happened, and I'm tapped out. Miller's re-booking them so I need, ya know, walkingaround money, just to--
They both start talking simultaneous, overlapping each other.

(overlapping) You got some fucking nerve. I don't see you. I don't hear from you. Nothing. I gotta read in Google News that you were arrested. Like I'm nothing to you. Like my family is nothing to you.
(overlapping) No listen, it's nothing. Look, it happened so fast. They take your cell phone away in prison. It was like OZ. Lotta naked men. I was very occupied staying single.
FLO, James' wife enters and the two brothers immediately stop talking. Flo commands the space.
Jackie. How nice you're here.
Hello Flo.
Happy Thanksgiving. We haven't seen you in how long?
I don't even remember.
I was in prison.
No it was long before that. So nice to see you.
How're the kids?
One kid. We got one kid.
She's an adult now.
She's getting married.
Really, Brit's getting married?
Brittany.
I thought she was a dyke.
You call'em lesbians, ya prick!
All right. Gay. Sorry.
And they get married. They can get married.
You don't have to come. (to James) He doesn't have to come.
We sent you an invitation. We didn't hear nothing.
I was in prison.
He's doesn't have to come if he's too busy. If it's too much trouble, don't come...
I didn't say it was too much trouble....
Jimmy starts to realize that Flo didn't send Jackine an

(overlapping) No, if it's too much trouble, don't come. I don't need the extra aggravation if it's gonna mean trouble for you. I don't need it to become a thing. (overlapping) I never said it's...It's not a thing.
(overlapping) I don't think it's a thing. (overlapping) If it's gonna be a thing, then don't bother.
(surrenders) IT'S NO TROUBLE! It's not a thing! I'd love to come. I'll be there. It's a week from Sunday.
(forcing herself) You want to stay and have Thanksgiving dinner with us after we close?
Jackie knows she's forcing the invite;
Not really. And I'll give ya a good excuse. I'm working the mission. I get double the hours taken off my sentence for working Thanksgiving.
God forbid you do it 'cause it's a nice thing.. (to James) I need to speak to you when you two are finished.
She exits. The two brothers look to each other.
She doesn't hate you. She doesn't?
Nah, I was just fucking with you. Of course she hates you. You only show up when you need something.
That's it? Sensitive bitch. None of my wives hated you.
You weren't married to any of the them long enough to meet me. (then) So how much do you need?
I don't even remember now. I'm exhausted.
Walks over and looks up to his father's portrait.
Pop liked me best. No he didn't.
No, he didn't. (then) Five thousand. No big deal. You know I'll get it back to you.
James writes out a check.
My daughter idolizes you. Her famous uncle. (hands him the check) You better show up.
And bring a gift.
Jackie hands back the check to James.
Okay, but that'll cost you extra.
INT. BURKOWITZ DELI - CASH REGISTER - DAY
Flo is speaking with KAROLA, a STATUESQUE German BLOND beautiful, but tough as nails.
We paid a $30,000 deposit.
Yes, and the second $30,000 is due today.
(digging in her heals) We'll pay the balance on the day of the ceremony.
(shaking her head)
James is walking Jackie out and recognizes Karola and turns on the charm.

Whoa, look who's here ... (kisses her hand) Karola, meet my famous brother, Jackie Burke (turns to Jackie) Jackie, this beautiful fraulein runs Da Mikele Illagio, where Brittany is getting hitched. Jackie - Karola Muller.
(a seductive smile) Oh, we already know each other.
Jackie looks at her quizzically
You don't remember?
(baffled) Sorry ...
You & me .. after one of your shows at Caroline's . . .
I haven't played Caroline's in ten years. (shaking his head) Sorry, I was drinking a lot in those days.
(knowingly) Yes you were . . .
(ducking past her) Sorry, gotta run -- big lunch date at the Bowery Mission. (stage whisper) The food is much better there. (then) Auf wiedersehen
Jackie runs out of the Deli. tries to smooth things over. Karola is miffed, but Jimmy
(turning to Karola) Can I get you anything? (seductively) Latka?
Cool it, Don Juan, the "Enforcer" is here to collect another 30 grand.
It's in the contract.
You think I read all 70 pages of that ferkakta contract.
(standing her ground) Yes, I expect your to.
(cuts her off) Florie, their check is in the register. Just give it to Karola.
Flo takes a CHECK our of the Cash Register.
(feigning surprise) Oh, here it is. At least you saved me a stamp.
Karola leans down and hugs Jimmy. He can't help looking into her ample cleavage. She stands back up and says:
Now, I'd like a bowl of your Matza Ball Soup.
Sure a big bowl. It costs $30 Grand.
EXT. THE BOWERY MISSION - THANKSGIVING DAY
A sign above the door: "He that cometh to me shall never hunger". Camera tracks down a long line of HOMELESS PEOPLE, mostly men, stretching down the sidewalk.
Growing up, my family observed all the Thanksgiving traditions. My favorite was my Uncle Moshit telling the Thanksgiving Story.
INT. THE BOWERY MISSION - CONTINUOUS - DAY

The cramped space is filled with hungry vets, hookers, trannies, etc...some still shivering from the cold. Jackie, wearing a black hair net underneath a sanitary hat, is dishing out food. While dishing out food, he speaks into a microphone on a stand;

Uncle Moshit told me that before the first Thanksgiving, Pilgrims thought turkeys were sex objects. They couldn't fly and "gobble gobble" was Puritan for "blowjob". When the Indians showed up - right after the Macy's Parade - they brought turkeys all trussed up. The Pilgrims were pissed. They were like "They're eating our fuck birds". But being Puritans, they didn't want to be rude. And it turned out, turkeys tasted better than going down on their wives.
Beyond the line of homeless, Jackie can't help notice HARMONY, refilling ice tea and cups of coffee - laughs at Jackie's routine along with the crowd.
So the Puritans stopped fucking the turkeys and started fucking the Indians instead. And that's how we stole their country and made the turkey our national bird.

Scattered applause and laughs. Jackie's eye follows Harmony across the room, filling the coffee cups of two vets as she talks to them. She's full of energy. Everyone seems to like her. Including Jackie.
INT. THE BOWERY MISSION - LATER THAT DAY

Wide Shot; Volunteers are cleaning up. Jackie is holdin g his paperwork. We see him ask one of the VOLUNTEERS where the supervisor is. The Volunteer points upstairs. Jackie walks up a flight of stairs to the chapel office in the balcony, featuring a stained glass window.
INT. CHAPEL OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
The office's door is ajar. Jackie pauses when he hears an one sided argument from inside the office:
YOU REALLY ARE A PIECE OF SHIT, GEORGE, YOU KNOW THAT...OH REALLY?...HOW IS THIS ALL MY FAULT NOW! TELL ME!..... Jackie inches the door open to see Harmony pacing behind a desk while on the phone.
OH PLEASE, I DIDN'T IT THROW AT HER, I WAS THROWING IT AT YOU AND SHE WALKED IN FRONT OF IT. THAT'S ON HER, THAT'S NOT ON ME. WHO DOES
HER? SO SHE'S NOT THERE! OH
FOR WHAT, GEORGE! FUCK YOU. FUCK
over? Slamming the phone down, she is startled by Jackie.
standing there listening to a
Sorry, they told me I need you to sign the paperwork for my hours. I'm not the supervisor. You want Bobbi. She's at the store.
Oh sorry, I just assumed-HARMONY I'm allowed to use the office! We don't get cell service in here. OK.
But don't say anything.
Nope. I won't say anything. I'm not messing with you, lady. I'm just glad I'm not George. What do you know about George? (protecting himself) He's a piece of shit! You got that right.
Jackie smirks, disarming Harmony a bit. She softens.
I know you.
Oh yeah? You're Jackie Burke. My Dad loved your show. I've seen every episode.
Always nice to meet a fan.
Not really. We only had one TV. This deflects Jackie for a moment. What's your name?
Harmony Schutz? Were your parents in a Nazi Barbershop Quartet? That's so funny.
(flirty) Well, you know, they say once you make a woman laugh, you can make her do anything.
Harmony stops laughing, more fascinated than insulted.
(interrupting) As in very, very old? End on Jackie - he's intrigues.
INT. JACKIE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

A nice, but cluttered two-bedroom apartment that hasn't been redecorated in decades. Lots of books, notebooks, videos. Through the window e can see the brick building next door. Jackie stands in his boxer shorts and a tee shirt, ironing a pair of pants. As he listens to Art Blakey's "Meet You At The Jazz Corner Of The World Vol. 2" As the famous midget, Pee Wee Marquette introduces the band, Jackie mouths the words perfectly:
Ladies & Gentlemen, we are now beginning the 3rd Set with the
terrific Art Blakey and his Jazz
The World. Lee Morgan on Trumpet, Wayne Shorter on the Tenor
EXT. COMEDY CELLAR - MACDOUGAL STREET - NIGHT
Rain pelts a Hip Crowd lined up on the sidewalk. Jackie passes them, nodding to the door man, and descending the funky stairs into this Comedy Mecca.
COMEDY CELLAR - UPSTAIRS BAR AREA - NIGHT

Walks into this mecca of Comedy. He approaches the 'comics table' where several well-known comics sit kibitzing. They ignore him - it's clear that there's no * room at this 'hip' table for Jackie. He moves on to:
INT. COMEDY CELLAR SHOWROOM - NIGHT
A BLACK COMEDIAN, GREER BARNS, tries out new material on a tiny stage. Jackie paces slowly in the back aisle, checking out the competition. He never laughs, occasionally nods approval.
